source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Cylinder C (mass 10.0 kg and radius 0.070 m) rolls without slipping on hill H as shown in Fig. 1.121. The string does not stretch and is wrapped around the cylinder C. How far vertically upward does C move when the 2 kg mass moves down one meter?

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Cylinder C (mass 10.0 kg and radius 0.070 m) rolls without slipping on hill H as shown in Fig. 1.121. The string does not stretch and is wrapped around the cylinder C. How far vertically upward does C move when the 2 kg mass moves down one meter?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a concrete file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: As the string is unstretchable, when the center of $C$ moves up the inclined plane a distance $\Delta x$, the 2 kg mass will also drop $\Delta x$. However, as an additional length $\Delta x$ of the string is also released in the process, the 2 kg mass will actually drop $2 \Delta x$. Thus when the 2 kg mass moves down one meter, $C$ will move up the inclined plane 0.5 m, or vertically up $0.5 \sin 30^{\circ}=0.25 \mathrm{~m}$.
